title: GitHub CLI Tool system
content:
GitHub CLI Bot System – Notes

Goal:
Create a CLI system that allows linking a GitHub account, browsing repositories, cloning projects, searching users/repos, and connecting local folders to repositories with merge or overwrite functionality.

---

GitHub Account Linking

- Use GitHub Personal Access Token (PAT), not username/password
- Prompt user to input token
- Validate token using GitHub API (/user endpoint)
- Store token securely using encryption (Fernet, not hashing)

Command:
- /github-link
- /github-unlink

---

View Repositories

- Fetch authenticated user repos using API
- Endpoint: /user/repos

Command:
- /repos

---

View Other Users / Repos

- Fetch public user info and repos

Commands:
- /user <username>
- /repos <username>

Endpoints:
- /users/{username}
- /users/{username}/repos

---

Search Repositories

- Use GitHub search API

Command:
- /search-repo <query>

Endpoint:
- /search/repositories?q=<query>

---

Clone Repositories

- Use system git command to clone repos locally

Command:
- /clone <repo_url>

Behavior:
- Clone into current or specified directory
- Option to rename folder after clone

---

Repository Connection System (Core Feature)

Purpose:
Allow a non-git folder to connect to an existing GitHub repo

---

Connection File

File name:
- .cli_bot_repo

Contents:
{
  "repo_url": "...",
  "linked": true,
  "mode": "add" or "replace"
}

Command:
- /repo-init <repo_url>

Behavior:
- Creates connection file
- Stores repo reference for future commands

---

Mode 1: Add (Merge into existing repo)

Command:
- /repo-connect add <repo_url>

Behavior:
- Initialize git in folder
- Connect to remote repo
- Pull existing repo with unrelated histories allowed
- Add local files
- Commit and push

Steps:
- git init
- git remote add origin <repo_url>
- git pull origin main --allow-unrelated-histories
- git add .
- git commit
- git push

Result:
- Keeps existing repo files
- Adds new folder contents

---

Mode 2: Replace (Overwrite repo)

Command:
- /repo-connect replace <repo_url>

Behavior:
- Initialize git
- Force push local files to repo

Steps:
- git init
- git remote add origin <repo_url>
- git add .
- git commit
- git branch -M main
- git push -f origin main

Result:
- Deletes all existing repo content
- Replaces with local folder

---

Auto Sync

Command:
- /repo-sync

Behavior:
- Read .cli_bot_repo file
- Automatically commit and push changes

Steps:
- git add .
- git commit -m "Auto sync"
- git push

---

System Structure

- commands/github.py
- utils/github_api.py
- utils/git_manager.py
- utils/encryption.py

---

Security

- Never store tokens in plain text
- Use encryption (Fernet)
- Limit token permissions (repo, read:user)

---

Summary

System includes:

- GitHub account linking
- Repo browsing (self + others)
- Repo search
- Repo cloning
- Folder-to-repo connection system
- Merge or overwrite modes
- Auto sync functionality
- Local connection file for persistent linking
